_note: Sanctioned statute-capture targets for scripts/statute_capture.py and the .github/workflows/statute-capture.yml manual workflow. This is the SINGLE source of truth for which OpenLeg sections the capture workflow may pull, and where each lands in golden-copy/sources/. Declaring a target here does NOT create a golden file or mark anything verified golden -- the workflow run fetches the section, writes a PENDING-HUMAN-READ candidate, and opens a PR for human review.
_freshness_note: freshness_check.py merges any target here whose golden file ALREADY EXISTS on disk into its monthly check (existence-guarded, so a pending target can never break the live run). This is how a captured section becomes 'freshness-registered' once its golden file merges.
_id_format: Keys are OpenLeg law coordinates 'LAWID/LOCATIONID' (uppercase law id; e.g. GCN/24, GCN/25-A, EXC/314). mode is 'new' (no stored golden; full-section capture) or 'existing' (diff-only against the stored golden). min_subdivisions is the fail-closed floor on parsed top-level subdivisions (default 1 = section body must be present and non-truncated).

GCN/24.subject: General Construction Law § 24 -- public holidays (payment-clock holiday source, PR 2)

GCN/25-A.subject: General Construction Law § 25-a -- act due on Saturday/Sunday/public holiday extends to next business day (payment-clock deadline source, PR 2)

EXC/314.subject: Executive Law § 314 -- MWBE statewide certification program (audit re-pull; follow-up B; expect FULL-MATCH)
